what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard item that records a one-breath summary of the completed five-agent system map, listing grounded answers for six topics and surfacing a convergence question about channels and dragnet engines.
observations: The graph, scopes, self/relative-root, dragnet engine, cards-as-observations, and channels/membership are each mapped to existing or declared-unbuilt machinery.
A unified walkable web is described as the system's own declared-unbuilt next step.
Scopes are carried as an address-frame ladder that still needs extension to dragnet outputs and a channel rung.
The dragnet engine has a frozen code-only gating rule, which caused a design stage to be skipped; two engines exist and one already has a design stage.
Cards already have mark, comment, and edge machinery; pointing that machinery at cards is the remaining work.
Three channel systems exist; membership gates delivery, not data access; the lead maps to the existing coordinator role; the default is that everyone can do everything.
The single new architectural question is whether the three channel systems and two dragnet engines must converge before clean ownership/access semantics can be added.
standouts: It frames the convergence of three channel systems and two dragnet engines as one unified architectural question.
It explains that a frozen code-only gating rule is why design work was skipped.
It maps the proposed lead role to the existing coordinator role and notes the permissive default reality.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item format with YAML front matter, id, address, type, source, channel, and a part_of link.
Uses bold labels to structure a bullet summary.
Title uses a metaphorical framing ('one breath').
Ends with an inferred architectural question rather than a decision request.
concerns: Terms such as dragnet, channel, scope ladder, and address frame are not defined inside this file.
The three channel systems and two dragnet engines are asserted but not enumerated.
The claim that parallel structures probably want to converge is speculative.
No owner, decision, or next step is attached to the convergence question.
The text assumes prior knowledge of the five-agent map and the addressed reader.
notes: A status snapshot of the five-agent map that flags channel/dragnet convergence as the next architectural blocker.
questions: What are the three channel systems and the two dragnet engines specifically?
What is the unified walkable web and how is it declared unbuilt?
What is the address-frame scope ladder and what would a channel rung look like?
What is the frozen code-only gating rule exactly?
What does cards-as-observations plus member feeding mean operationally?
Who is the 'you' addressed in the text?
purpose_vs_actual: The title says it presents the completed five-agent map in one breath; the body does that and additionally reframes the remaining work as a convergence problem, which is consistent but goes beyond a pure status report.
apparent_themes: Converge parallel structures before adding ownership and access semantics.
Ground design claims in existing running machinery.
Distinguish delivery gating from data access.
Treat permissive defaults as current reality rather than desired policy.
intention_signals: To consolidate five-agent findings into a single grounded snapshot, show that most concerns are already addressed, and elevate channel/dragnet convergence as the prerequisite for the next design move.
novelty: It explicitly pairs three channel systems and two dragnet engines as the same kind of convergence problem, arguing that they must unify before clean channel-owned-dragnet/member-read semantics can be established.
contribution: A concise cross-cutting synthesis that maps proposed concepts to existing or pending machinery and identifies channel/dragnet convergence as a blocking prerequisite for ownership and access design.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item summarizing the current state of a five-agent system map, mapping each proposed concept to existing or pending machinery and identifying the convergence of three channel systems and two dragnet engines as the key architectural prerequisite.
intention_for_embedding: To consolidate five-agent findings into a single grounded snapshot and surface channel/dragnet convergence as the next design blocker.
